prompt.task:
Ensure consistent and controllable behavior of the AI model by introducing a system prompt that defines tone, personality, and response style.
## Context Currently, the chatbot responds without a fixed behavioral guideline, which leads to inconsistent tone and style. Introducing a system prompt will standardize responses and improve user experience.
## Requirements - Add a system prompt that defines model behavior (e.g., "You are an experienced programmer who answers concisely and clearly" or "You are a friendly old companion"). - Include the system prompt as part of the message context sent to the LLM. - Ensure the system prompt is always the first message in the conversation payload. - Make the system prompt configurable (optional but recommended).
## Implementation Notes - Use the standard message format: [ { "role": "system", "content": "..." }, { "role": "user", "content": "..." }, { "role": "assistant", "content": "..." } ] - Inject the system prompt before user history when calling the LLM. - Ensure compatibility with existing history handling logic. - Avoid duplicating the system prompt on every request if already present.
## Definition of Done - [ ] System prompt is added to LLM requests - [ ] Model responses reflect defined behavior (tone/style) - [ ] System prompt is consistently applied across all conversations - [ ] No regressions in existing chat functionality - [ ] Code is clean and maintainable
## Affected Files / Components - src/llm.py - src/prompts.py (if exists) - src/handlers.py
## Risks / Dependencies - Incorrect placement of system prompt may reduce effectiveness - Conflicts with existing prompt engineering logic
## Validation Steps 1. Send a test prompt without system prompt → observe baseline behavior 2. Enable system prompt and resend the same input 3. Compare responses and verify tone/style changes 4. Test across multiple inputs to ensure consistency
## Follow-ups (optional) - Add support for dynamic system prompt per user - Store system prompt configuration externally (e.g., config file or DB)
